mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate suppresses the risk-free discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rebalance toward risk assets.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls exceeding 100,000 signals deteriorating household income and aggregate demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when PAYEMS declines exceed 100,000.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between month-over-month changes in retail sales and housing starts captures broad demand momentum, and simultaneous weakness in both series amplifies negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST monthly changes fails to show a statistically significant negative relation with 60-day S&P 500 returns at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned near zero, an acceleration in consumer price inflation reduces the real cost of capital and lifts nominal revenue growth expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months when CPIAUCSL rises while FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization that translate directly into higher corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month INDPRO changes are followed by negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding 1% signals a sharp contraction in consumer spending that reduces corporate revenue forecasts, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances when RSAFS declines exceed 1% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with a federal funds rate near zero indicates labor market deterioration without conventional monetary space to offset it, causing investors to demand a higher equity risk premium and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: constrained monetary policy with rising unemployment
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 50% of observations when UNRATE rises while FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals manufacturing recession and negative operating leverage, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO declines exceed 0.5% month-over-month.
